title: Rich Silky Filipino Semolina Halo Halo
description: A charred, silky Filipino dessert featuring semolina, nimono-simmer, and ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly ingredients, served in a halo-halo form.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a saucepan, combine the semolina and 2 cups of water, and cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens.
In a separate saucepan, combine the coconut milk, palm sugar, and 1/4 cup of water, and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar dissolves.
Add the jackfruit, kaong, and ube halaya to the coconut milk mixture, and cook for 5 minutes.
Char the saba banana over an open flame or in a dry skillet until the skin is blackened.
Peel the banana and slice it into rounds.
To assemble the halo-halo, place a scoop of the semolina mixture in a bowl, followed by a spoonful of the coconut milk mixture, some sliced banana, and a sprinkle of pinipig.
Repeat the layers one more time, ending with a sprinkle of pinipig on top.
Serve the halo-halo chilled, garnished with additional pinipig and ube halaya if desired.
